description: Posterior circulation stroke presenting as isolated dizziness and nausea. No FAST positive. Atypical stroke presentation that misses standard stroke screening.

patient.name: Helen J., 68F
patient.chief_complaint: Sudden onset severe dizziness and nausea 90 minutes ago. Cannot walk straight, room spinning. Brief blurred vision earlier. No headache, no facial droop, no arm weakness, normal speech. Spouse drove her in.
patient.history: Atrial fibrillation on apixaban, HTN, hyperlipidemia, type 2 diabetes

rubric_notes: **CRITICAL MISS TEST.** Vitals look benign-to-concerning (BP 168 borderline). NO classic FAST positives. BUT: 68-year-old with afib + sudden vertigo + ataxia + transient visual disturbance = POSTERIOR CIRCULATION STROKE (vertebrobasilar) until proven otherwise. Cardioembolic risk from afib is massive. Anterior FAST screen misses posterior strokes. The agent must recognize the 'HiNTS exam' demographic + risk profile and elevate to ESI 1-2 despite negative FAST. ESI 4 here = missing a stroke in the tPA window. Need MRI (CT misses posterior fossa) + neurology consult. Posterior strokes are MORE deadly than anterior because of brainstem proximity.
